question: How much will the policy pay for road ambulance charges?
answer:
The policy will pay the reasonable cost to a maximum of Rs 20,000/- per policy year incurred on an ambulance for transferring You to the nearest Hospital with adequate emergency facilities for the provision of health services following an Emergency. [Silver, section "4. Road Ambulance", page 2]

The policy will also reimburse expenses incurred on an ambulance for transferring You from the Hospital where you were admitted initially to another hospital with higher medical facilities. [Silver, section "4. Road Ambulance", page 2]

question: How many days of medical expenses before hospitalisation are covered?
answer:
Medical expenses incurred during the 60 days immediately before hospitalization are covered, provided they were for the same illness/injury for which subsequent hospitalization was required and an inpatient hospitalization claim has been accepted [Gold, section "2. Pre-Hospitalisation", page 1].

Medical expenses incurred immediately before hospitalization are covered, provided they are for the same condition for which hospitalization was required and the inpatient hospitalization claim is admissible [Gold, section "46. Pre-hospitalization Medical Expenses", page 8].

question: How many days of medical expenses after discharge from hospital are covered?
answer: The Medical Expenses incurred during the 90 days immediately after You were discharged post Hospitalisation are covered, provided that such costs are incurred in respect of the same illness/injury for which the earlier Hospitalisation was required, and an inpatient Hospitalisation claim has been accepted under Inpatient Hospitalisation Treatment. [Silver, section "3. Post-Hospitalisation", page 2]

question: Is there any limit on room rent?
answer:
Yes, there is a limit on room rent for Ayurvedic/Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses. The maximum liability is up to Rs. 20,000 per policy year for this benefit. [Gold, section "11. Ayurvedic / Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses", page 2]

For In-patient Hospitalisation Treatment, there is no sublimit for Room, Boarding and Nursing Expenses as provided by the Hospital/Nursing Home. [Gold, section "1. In-patient Hospitalisation Treatment", page 1]

question: Is there any limit on room rent?
answer: Yes, there is a limit on room rent. For In-patient Hospitalisation Treatment, Room, Boarding and Nursing Expenses are covered up to 1% of the Sum Insured per day (Excluding Cumulative Bonus) or the actual expenses, whichever is lower [Silver, section "1. In-patient Hospitalisation Treatment", page 2].

question: What is the waiting period for cataract surgery?
answer: The waiting period for cataract surgery is 24 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first Health Guard Policy with Us. [Gold, section "2. Specified disease/procedure waiting period (Excl02) (part 1)", page 9]

question: What is the waiting period for pre-existing diseases?
answer:
The waiting period for pre-existing diseases is 36 months of continuous coverage after the date of inception of the first Health Guard Policy with Bajaj Allianz. [Gold, section "1. Pre-existing Diseases waiting period (Excl01)", page 8]

If the Sum Insured is enhanced, the exclusion will apply afresh to the extent of the increase. [Gold, section "1. Pre-existing Diseases waiting period (Excl01)", page 8]

If the Insured has continuous coverage without any break as per portability norms, the waiting period will be reduced by the extent of prior coverage. [Gold, section "1. Pre-existing Diseases waiting period (Excl01)", page 8]

Coverage for any pre-existing disease after 36 months is subject to it being declared at the time of application and accepted by Bajaj Allianz. [Gold, section "1. Pre-existing Diseases waiting period (Excl01)", page 8]

A pre-existing disease is defined as any condition, ailment, injury, or disease diagnosed by a physician or for which medical advice or treatment was recommended or received from a physician within 48 months prior to the effective date of the policy or its reinstatement. [Gold, section "45. Pre-Existing Disease", page 8]

When a new member is added to the policy, the pre-existing disease clause and waiting periods will be applicable considering that Policy Year as the first year for that insured member. [Gold, section "29. Inclusion of members under the policy:", page 16]

question: How long do I have to wait before maternity expenses are covered?
answer:
The waiting period for maternity expenses is 72 months from the date of issuance of the first policy with us, provided that the policy has been continuously renewed with us without a break. [Gold, section "12. Maternity Expenses", page 3]

Any treatment arising from or traceable to pregnancy, child birth including cesarean section and/or any treatment related to pre and postnatal care and complications arising out of Pregnancy and Childbirth until 72 months continuous period has elapsed since the inception of the first Health Guard Policy with US. [Gold, section "Waiting Period for Maternity Expenses", page 9]

A fresh waiting period of 72 months would apply for all policies issued with continuity under portability guidelines, either from our existing Health Product or any other Non-Health or Standalone Health Insurance Company. [Gold, section "12. Maternity Expenses", page 3]

question: Is any illness covered in the first month of a brand new policy?
answer: Expenses related to the treatment of any illness within 30 days from the first Policy commencement date shall be excluded except claims arising due to an accident, provided the same are covered [Gold, section "4. 30-day waiting period (Excl03)", page 9]. This exclusion shall not apply if the Insured has Continuous Coverage for more than twelve months [Gold, section "4. 30-day waiting period (Excl03)", page 9].

question: Is bariatric surgery covered and what are the conditions?
answer:
Yes, bariatric surgery is covered under the Gold plan, subject to certain conditions.

Conditions for Bariatric Surgery Cover:
*   **Definition:** Bariatric surgery means surgery on the stomach and/or intestines to help a person with extreme obesity lose weight. It is an option for people with a BMI above 40, or a BMI between 35 and 40 who also have health problems like type 2 diabetes or heart disease. [Gold, section "8. Bariatric surgery:", page 4]
*   **Waiting Period:** There is a waiting period of 36 months from the date of the first commencement of the policy and continuous renewal thereof. A fresh waiting period of 36 months applies for policies issued with continuity under portability guidelines. [Gold, section "Special Conditions applicable to Bariatric Surgery Cover", page 3]
*   **Medical Advice and Approval:** The surgery must be conducted upon the advice of a Doctor and supported by clinical protocols. [Gold, section "General Exclusions (part 2)", page 10] The indication for the procedure should be found appropriate by two qualified surgeons, and the insured person must obtain prior approval for cashless treatment from the Company. [Gold, section "Special Conditions applicable to Bariatric Surgery Cover", page 3]
*   **Age and BMI Requirements:** The member must be 18 years of age or older. [Gold, section "General Exclusions (part 2)", page 10] The Body Mass Index (BMI) must be:
    *   greater than or equal to 40, or
    *   greater than or equal to 35 in conjunction with any of the following severe co-morbidities following failure of less invasive methods of weight loss: Obesity-related cardiomyopathy, Coronary heart disease, Severe Sleep Apnea, or Uncontrolled Type2 Diabetes. [Gold, section "General Exclusions (part 2)", page 10]
*   **Exclusions:** Bariatric surgery performed for cosmetic reasons is excluded. [Gold, section "Special Conditions applicable to Bariatric Surgery Cover", page 3] Expenses related to the surgical treatment of obesity that does not fulfill all the specified conditions are excluded. [Gold, section "General Exclusions (part 2)", page 10]
*   **Company's Liability:** The maximum liability is restricted to 50% of the Sum Insured, up to a maximum of Rs. 5 lac. [Gold, section "Special Conditions applicable to Bariatric Surgery Cover", page 3] and [Gold, section "12. Basis of Claims Payment (part 1)", page 13]

question: What is the email address of the insurance ombudsman office in Hyderabad?
answer: The email address for the Insurance Ombudsman office in Hyderabad is bimalokpal.hyderabad@ecoi.co.in [Gold, section "Grievance Redressal Cell for Senior Citizens (part 4)", page 17].

question: Is there a dedicated phone number for senior citizens?
answer:
Yes, there is a dedicated health toll-free number for senior citizens.

For the Gold plan: 1800-103-2529 [Gold, section "Grievance Redressal Cell for Senior Citizens (part 1)", page 17]
For the Silver plan: 1800-103-2529 [Silver, section "Grievance Redressal Cell for Senior Citizens (part 1)", page 15]

question: What discount do I get if I buy the policy for 2 years?
answer: If the policy is opted for 2 years, a 4% discount is applicable. [Gold, section "iv. Long Term Policy Discount: + Zone A", page 16] This discount will not apply to policies where the premium is paid in installments. [Gold, section "iv. Long Term Policy Discount: + Zone A", page 16]

question: How much daily cash benefit is paid when a parent stays with an insured child in hospital?
answer: The Daily Cash Benefit paid for reasonable accommodation expenses for one parent/legal guardian to stay with a minor Insured Person (under the Age of 12) is Rs. 500 per day, maximum up to 10 days during each policy year. This is applicable if the hospitalization claim is paid under Section A1 Inpatient Hospitalisation Treatment and the policy term is more than 1 year. [Silver, section "8. Daily Cash Benefit for Accompanying an Insured Child", page 2]

question: Does the policy cover treatment taken outside India?
answer: No, any treatment received outside India is not covered under this Policy [Gold, section "General Exclusions (part 5)", page 10]. We cover insured events arising during the Policy Period, as well as treatment availed, within India only [Gold, section "30. Territorial Limits & Governing Law", page 16].

question: Which hospitals in Mumbai are part of the cashless network?
answer: The provided extracts do not contain a list of specific hospitals in Mumbai that are part of the cashless network. However, it is stated that cashless facility is offered through network hospitals of Bajaj Allianz only, with over 3300+ network hospitals PAN India. You can visit the website www.bajajallianz.com for a list of network hospitals. [Gold, section "Grievance Redressal Cell for Senior Citizens (part 8)", page 17], [Silver, section "Grievance Redressal Cell for Senior Citizens (part 15)", page 15]

question: What is the grace period for paying an instalment premium?
answer: The grace period for paying an instalment premium is 15 days. [Gold, section "6. Premium Payment in Instalments (Wherever applicable)", page 11]

question: How long is the waiting period for joint replacement surgery?
answer: The waiting period for joint replacement surgery is until the expiry of 3 consecutive annual periods during which you have the benefit of a Health Guard Policy with Us. [Gold, section "2. Specified disease/procedure waiting period (Excl02) (part 1)", page 9]

question: Is Ayurvedic treatment covered by the policy?
answer:
Yes, Ayurvedic treatment is covered by the policy under certain conditions.

For Ayurvedic/Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses, if you are hospitalised for not less than 24 hours in an Ayurvedic/Homeopathic Hospital (which is a government hospital or an institute recognized by the government or accredited by Quality Council of India/National Accreditation Board on Health) on the advice of a doctor due to illness or accidental bodily injury sustained or contracted during the policy period, the policy will cover medical expenses for in-patient Ayurvedic and Homeopathic treatment. This includes room rent, boarding expenses, nursing care, consultation fees, medicines, drugs and consumables, and Ayurvedic and Homeopathic treatment procedures. [Gold, section "11. Ayurvedic / Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses", page 2]

The maximum liability for this benefit is up to Rs. 20,000 per policy year. [Gold, section "11. Ayurvedic / Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses", page 2] This benefit is applicable annually for policies with a term of more than 1 year. [Gold, section "11. Ayurvedic / Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses", page 2]

For a claim to be admissible, the illness/injury must require inpatient admission, and the procedure performed on the insured cannot be carried out on an out-patient basis. [Gold, section "11. Ayurvedic / Homeopathic Hospitalisation Expenses", page 2]

An AYUSH Hospital is defined as a healthcare facility where medical/surgical/para-surgical treatment procedures and interventions are carried out by AYUSH Medical Practitioner(s). This includes Central or State Government AYUSH Hospitals, teaching hospitals attached to AYUSH Colleges recognized by the Central Government/Central Council of Indian Medicine/Central Council for Homeopathy, or AYUSH Hospitals registered with local authorities, having at least 5 in-patient beds, qualified AYUSH Medical Practitioner(s) in charge round the clock, dedicated AYUSH therapy sections or equipped operation theatres, and maintaining daily patient records accessible to the insurance company. [Gold, section "4. AYUSH Hospital:", page 4]

Additionally, an AYUSH Day Care Centre is defined as a registered health centre with facilities for treatment procedures and medical or surgical/para-surgical interventions under the supervision of registered AYUSH Medical Practitioner(s) on a day care basis without in-patient services. [Gold, section "5. AYUSH Day Care Centre:", page 4]

question: How much is the convalescence benefit and when is it paid?
answer: The convalescence benefit is Rs. 5,000 per policy year. [Silver, section "7. Convalescence Benefit:", page 2] This benefit is paid in the event of the insured being hospitalized for a disease/illness/injury for a continuous period exceeding 10 days, provided the hospitalization claim is accepted under Section A1-In Patient Hospitalisation Treatment. [Silver, section "7. Convalescence Benefit:", page 2] This benefit is applicable annually for policies with a term of more than 1 year. [Silver, section "7. Convalescence Benefit:", page 2]

question: Do I get a free health checkup with this policy?
answer: Yes, you are eligible for a free Preventive Health checkup at the end of every block of 3 continuous policy years. [Gold, section "10. Preventive Health Check Up", page 2] The reimbursement will be an amount equal to 1% of the sum insured, with a maximum of Rs. 5000/- for each member in an Individual policy during the 3-year block. [Gold, section "10. Preventive Health Check Up", page 2] This benefit can be availed by the proposer and spouse only under Floater Sum Insured Policies. [Gold, section "10. Preventive Health Check Up", page 2] The company will not be liable for any other ancillary or peripheral costs or expenses, such as transportation, accommodation, or sustenance. [Gold, section "10. Preventive Health Check Up", page 2]

question: Which cities are included in Zone A for premium payment?
answer: The cities included in Zone A for premium payment are: Delhi / NCR, Mumbai including (Navi Mumbai, Thane and Kalyan), Hyderabad and Secunderabad, Bangalore, Kolkata, Ahmedabad, Vadodara and Surat. [Gold, section "27. Premium payment Zone", page 16]

question: Are organ donor expenses covered?
answer: Yes, organ donor expenses are covered. The policy will pay for expenses related to the organ donor's treatment for harvesting the donated organ, provided that the organ donor is compliant with THE TRANSPLANTATION OF HUMAN ORGANS (AMENDMENT) BILL, 2011, the organ is for the use of the Insured Person, and an inpatient hospitalization claim for the insured member has been accepted under In Patient Hospitalisation Treatment [Gold, section "6. Organ Donor Expenses:", page 2].

question: Are cosmetic dental implants covered under dental treatment?
answer: Cosmetic dental implants are not covered under dental treatment. [Gold, section "18. Dental Treatment + 19. Dependent child", page 5]
